summary: Drew DeVault's March 2021 status update covers progress on several smaller projects alongside his secret project and SourceHut. He built a Gemini feed reader available as both free software and a hosted service, added Gemini hosting support to pages.sr.ht, and switched gmni to use BearSSL. The godocs.io project also saw improvements with cruft removal and progress toward Go modules support.
key_points: Created a Gemini protocol feed reader, released as both free software and a free hosted service
Added Gemini hosting support to pages.sr.ht and switched gmni from OpenSSL to BearSSL
godocs.io improvements focused on removing obsolete code, making the fork lighter and easier to self-host
Collaborative open source work with adnano highlighted as key to progress across multiple projects
money_quotes: After the brief illusion of spring, this morning meets us with a cold apartment indoors and fierce winds outdoors.
Heaps of obsolete interfaces and cruft have been excised, not only making it lighter for godocs.io, but also making our gddo fork much easier for you to run yourself.
key_insight: DeVault's steady investment in Gemini protocol tooling and self-hostable infrastructure reflects his commitment to building decentralized alternatives to the corporate web.
